response_raw:
{
"mode": "human",
"topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
"opinion": "To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is important to be aware of the potential for false information and to verify the accuracy of what you see. This can involve fact-checking and cross-referencing information from multiple sources. Additionally, using reputable social media platforms and fact-checking organizations can help to reduce the amount of fake content that appears on your feed. It is also important to be mindful of your own biases and to seek out diverse perspectives to ensure a well-rounded understanding of the issues. Finally, it is crucial to be proactive in reporting and flagging fake content when you encounter it, so that it can be removed from the platform and prevented from spreading further."
,"reasoning_summary": "This answer is based on the following reasoning: 1) Be aware of potential for false information and verify accuracy by fact-checking and cross-referencing. 2) Use reputable platforms and fact-checking orgs to reduce fake content. 3) Be mindful of biases, seek diverse perspectives. 4) Proactively report/flag fake content.",

}

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is important to be aware of the potential for false information and to verify the accuracy of what you see. This can involve fact-checking and cross-referencing information from multiple sources. Additionally, using reputable social media platforms and fact-checking organizations can help to reduce the amount of fake content that appears on your feed. It is also important to be mindful of your own biases and to seek out diverse perspectives to ensure a well-rounded understanding of the issues. Finally, it is crucial to be proactive in reporting and flagging fake content when you encounter it, so that it can be removed from the platform and prevented from spreading further.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: This answer is based on the following reasoning: 1) Be aware of potential for false information and verify accuracy by fact-checking and cross-referencing. 2) Use reputable platforms and fact-checking orgs to reduce fake content. 3) Be mindful of biases, seek diverse perspectives. 4) Proactively report/flag fake content.
